scenario: Happy path — readiness report verdict is clean, all Pre-Delivery Actions are already done, the next undelivered UC has spec and design in place, and delivery is delegated to /deliver-use-case without abbreviation.
preconditions: Target project's CLAUDE.md contains the Nexa Rules Gate marker
Current git branch is sprint-1 (Sprint Branch Gate passes)
docs/sprints/next-sprint/readiness-report.md exists with '## Verdict' = READY (not BLOCKERS REMAIN)
The readiness report's Pre-Delivery Actions table lists a Prisma migration action, and prisma/schema.prisma already contains the entities from the Entity Model Changes section
The readiness report's Gaps section has no warning-severity entries
Delivery Order table lists UC-A (row 1) and UC-B (row 2); docs/delivery/UC-A-iterations.md does not yet exist for either
expected_steps: Nexa Rules Gate and Sprint Branch Gate both pass
Step 2: readiness report read; Verdict confirmed READY; Prisma migration Pre-Delivery Action confirmed satisfied by inspecting prisma/schema.prisma; no warnings to surface

Step 3: Delivery Order walked from row #1 downward; UC-A selected as the first row with no docs/delivery/UC-A-iterations.md; progress displayed as '0 / 2 use cases', 'Previously delivered this sprint: None'
Step 4: docs/use_cases/UC-A.md and docs/designs/UC-A-design.html both confirmed present
Step 5: /deliver-use-case UC-A invoked, following the full deliver-use-case SKILL.md pipeline without abbreviation
expected_terminal_outcome: sprint-deliver hands off cleanly to the /deliver-use-case pipeline for UC-A with no warnings or blockers surfaced; the progress summary correctly reports 0/2 delivered before this invocation.
pass_criteria: No Pre-Delivery Action or Gaps warning is incorrectly surfaced when the readiness report shows none; the correct next-undelivered UC (first row without an iterations log) is selected; /deliver-use-case is invoked for exactly that UC with its full pipeline, not a shortened version.
